Front Doors.Body: You stand before the glass front doors of the laundromat, but there is no street outside. Only a thick, grey fog presses against the glass, slick with condensation. The aggressive buzz of the fluorescent lights above makes your teeth ache. You try to push the doors open, but there are no handles on the inside. You are sealed in.

Aisle 1.Body: Rows of identical washing machines surround you. Most of them are running, the water sloshing rhythmically inside, but peering through the glass doors reveals they are completely empty. The air here is thick, humid, and reeks of cheap, artificial lavender detergent.

Aisle 2.Body: The buzzing of the overhead lights is slightly out of sync here, creating a nauseating strobe effect on the cracked linoleum floor. You notice a set of damp footprints leading down the aisle that don't belong to you. The sloshing from the surrounding machines sounds almost like whispering when you aren't looking directly at them.

Vending Corner.Body: Tucked between a humming, empty soda cooler and a broken change machine is an ancient arcade cabinet. The decals are worn away to a grey nothingness. It is entirely unplugged, its thick black cord severed on the floor, yet the screen emits a sickly, pulsating green glow.
ArcadeScreens: W A K E U P - 1111

Washer 4.Body: Unlike the others, Washer 4 is dead, its door hanging open. The smell of mildew and stagnant water is overpowering here. Near the bottom is a dark, damp slot—the lint trap. It looks unusually deep, and an unnatural cold draft seems to be seeping out of it.

Back Hallway.Body: The harsh fluorescent lighting fails to reach this narrow, claustrophobic corridor. The air is stagnant and freezing. At the end of the hall is a heavy, rusted metal door marked 'MANAGER'. It is jammed completely shut, secured by a blank metal faceplate. There is a single screw missing from the plate, leaving a small, flat slit just wide enough for something small.

Choices.Text: Wedge the Bent Quarter into the faceplate slit and pry open the Manager's Office door

Manager's Office.Body: You pry the metal plate off, the rusted screws snapping, and shove the heavy door open. You step inside, expecting a way out. Instead, you find a tiny, windowless concrete room. On a rusted metal desk sits a single security monitor, glowing brightly in the dark. It shows a live, black-and-white feed of the Front Doors. A tall, shadowy figure is standing right outside the foggy glass, waiting patiently to be let in.
